title: Ariadne DWG Graph IR v1
description: Engine-neutral intermediate representation of a DWG/DXF drawing as a graph of symbol-table records, block definitions, block references (with transforms), and per-entity geometry. Produced by any extraction lane (native ObjectARX, ObjectDBX, managed accoremgd, or DXF/ezdxf) and normalized to a single shape so downstream consumers (diff, validation, lineage) never depend on the producing engine. additionalProperties is true at object boundaries where richer engines may attach extra fields; the truth gate is diagnostics.entity_count vs the realized entities[] length.

ir_version.description: Optional semantic version of the IR producer, e.g. 1.0.0.

coverage_level.description: Declared completeness tier of this IR document. native_full = native/managed full traversal (header+symbol tables+blocks+entities+xdata+dictionaries). dbx_full = ObjectDBX side-database traversal. dxf_geometry = DXF-derived geometry-only (no live runtime objects/proxies). geometry_only = entities + bbox without symbol-table depth. partial = explicitly incomplete (see diagnostics.errors).

database.description: Drawing-level database state: header system variables, units, base point, extents, and global settings that scope the whole drawing.

header_vars.description: Raw DWG/DXF header system variables (e.g. ACADVER, DWGCODEPAGE, LUNITS, AUNITS, PDMODE). Keys are variable names; values are scalar/array as stored.

insbase.description: INSBASE header system variable: the drawing insertion base point used when this drawing is inserted as a block/xref into another.

ucs.description: Current UCS origin and axes at extraction time.

codepage.description: DWGCODEPAGE / drawing code page (relevant for non-ASCII layer names such as Korean text).

symbol_tables.description: The DWG symbol tables and named-object dictionaries. layers is the only required member (it may be empty for geometry_only coverage); all other tables are present at richer coverage levels.

text_styles.description: Text style (STYLE) table records, including SHX/TTF font binding.

block_table_records.description: Block table records (BTR): the named-block registry including *Model_Space, *Paper_Space, and anonymous blocks. Geometry of each block lives under block_definitions keyed by the same handle.

viewports.description: VPORT table records (named model-space viewport configurations). Paper-space VIEWPORT entities are carried in entities[] with class VIEWPORT.

ucs_table.description: UCS table records (named user coordinate systems).

app_ids.description: Registered application IDs (APPID / RegApp) used by XDATA.

block_definitions.description: Full geometric/content definition of each block table record. One entry per BTR that owns geometry; the entities owned by the block may be inlined here (def_entities) or referenced by owner_handle from the top-level entities[] array (an extractor picks one strategy and declares it in diagnostics.coverage).

block_references.description: Optional dedicated index of INSERT entities (block references) with resolved transforms and attribute values. INSERTs also appear in entities[]; this array is a convenience projection for consumers that traverse instance trees. May be omitted when entities[] is the single source.

xrefs.description: External reference (xref) block records: attached/overlaid drawings, their resolution status and saved path.

dictionaries.description: Named object dictionaries (ACAD_GROUP, ACAD_LAYOUT, ACAD_MLINESTYLE, ACAD_PLOTSETTINGS, custom app dictionaries, ...). Entries reference owned objects by handle.

extension_dictionaries.description: Per-object extension dictionaries (the dictionary an entity/object owns to attach app-specific persistent data). Keyed by owner_handle.

xrecords.description: XRECORD objects (resbuf/DXF-coded persistent records) wherever they live (in named dictionaries, extension dictionaries, or NOD). Each carries its owner and decoded result-buffer list.

groups.description: ACAD_GROUP entries: named/anonymous groups and their member entity handles.

members.description: Handles of entities that belong to the group.

plot_settings.description: Named plot settings (ACAD_PLOTSETTINGS) and per-layout plot configuration (paper size, plot area, scale).

layouts.description: Layout objects (ACAD_LAYOUT): Model + each paper-space layout, with their owning block table record handle and tab order.

block_table_record_handle.description: Handle of the paper-space BTR that holds this layout's geometry.

custom_objects.description: Custom/proxy objects and entities: ACAD_PROXY_ENTITY / ACAD_PROXY_OBJECT and live custom classes whose owning ARX app may or may not be loaded. Captures class name, proxy flags, and graphics-only fallback so a no-fake-success extraction can record 'present but not natively decoded'.

classes.description: DWG CLASSES section: registered custom class records (dxf_name, cpp_class_name, app_name, proxy flags). Lets a consumer know which entity classes require which ARX app.

entities.description: Flat list of all entities across model space and paper-space layouts (and optionally block-definition geometry, when not inlined under block_definitions). The realized length of this array is the truth-gate numerator; it MUST equal diagnostics.entity_count.

spatial_index.description: Optional spatial acceleration structure over entities[] (e.g. an R-tree / grid). Advisory only; consumers may ignore it. When absent, consumers compute their own index from per-entity bbox.

nodes.description: Index nodes; each typically references a bbox and a list of entity handles.

handle.description: DWG entity/object handle as an uppercase hex string (e.g. "2A7"). Stable across a single drawing's lifetime; the primary cross-engine join key in the IR.

bbox.description: Axis-aligned bounding box as [minX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ]. For 2D geometry minZ=maxZ=0. Empty array [] means 'no bbox computed' (e.g. for zero-extent or non-graphical objects).

source_descriptor.description: Provenance of this IR: which drawing file it came from, which engine/extractor produced it, and integrity fingerprints. Required so every IR is traceable to an exact input.

dwg_path.description: Path to the source DWG/DXF. For staged extraction this is the staged copy, not the read-only original.

original_path.description: Path to the read-only original, if the extraction worked from a staged copy.

dwg_version.description: DWG format version string, e.g. AC1032 (2018) / AC1027 (2013).

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the source file bytes for integrity/traceability.

extractor.description: Name of the producing extractor (e.g. objectarx_active_document, objectdbx_sidedb, autolisp_ssget, accoremgd_managed, ezdxf).

engine_tier.description: Which API surface produced this IR.

text_style_record.description: One STYLE (text style) symbol-table record, including font binding.

dim_style_record.description: One DIMSTYLE symbol-table record. Dimension variables (DIMSCALE, DIMTXT, ...) are carried in dim_vars.

dim_vars.description: Resolved dimension system variables for this style.

block_table_record.description: One BLOCK_RECORD (BTR) entry: the registry-level record for a block, model space, paper space, or anonymous block.

name.description: Block name. *Model_Space and *Paper_Space are the layout BTRs; *U### / *D### are anonymous.

entity_count.description: Number of entities owned by this BTR (definition geometry).

block_definition.description: Geometric definition of a block: its BTR handle/name plus either inlined definition entities or a pointer (via owner_handle on entities[]) to its geometry.

attribute_definitions.description: ATTDEF templates defined in the block.

def_entities.description: Inlined definition geometry (optional). When present these are the same entity shape as top-level entities[]; when absent, definition geometry is found in entities[] by owner_handle == this BTR handle.

block_reference.description: An INSERT (block reference) with resolved placement transform and any attribute values. Mirrors the corresponding entities[] item (same handle).

rotation.description: Rotation in radians (or degrees if declared by extractor; document in DWG_GRAPH_IR_SPEC).

attributes.description: ATTRIB values attached to this reference.

transform4x4.description: Block reference placement as a 4x4 row-major transform matrix (16 numbers). Provided alongside insertion_point/scale/rotation for consumers that prefer a single matrix.

dictionary_record.description: A DICTIONARY object and its entries.

name.description: Dictionary name as keyed in its parent (e.g. ACAD_LAYOUT).

value_handle.description: Handle of the object this key points to.

xrecord_record.description: An XRECORD object with its decoded result-buffer (resbuf) list.

dictionary.description: Owning dictionary name/path if applicable (e.g. ARIADNE, ARIADNE_NATIVE).

resbuf.description: Decoded result buffer: ordered DXF-coded values.

resbuf_item.description: One DXF group-code/value pair from a result buffer or XDATA.

value.description: Scalar value (number, string, handle, or point array) per the group code.

xdata_block.description: Extended entity data for one registered application.

viewport_record.description: A VPORT table record (named viewport config) or a paper-space VIEWPORT entity summary.

custom_object_record.description: A custom/proxy object or entity. Records enough to surface 'present but possibly not natively decodable' so extraction never fakes a decode it could not perform.

class_name.description: Runtime/DXF class name (e.g. ACAD_PROXY_ENTITY or a third-party class).

is_proxy.description: True when the owning ARX app is not loaded and only proxy graphics are available.

app_name.description: ARX application that owns this class, if known.

decoded.description: True only when the object was natively decoded. False = graphics/proxy fallback only (no-fake-success marker).

space.description: Which space the entity lives in.

geometry.description: Geometric payload of an entity, discriminated by kind. Only the fields relevant to a given kind are populated; additionalProperties true allows engine-specific extras (e.g. bulge arrays, OCS normals, spline control points). kind == unsupported means the extractor recognized the entity but did not emit coordinate geometry (no-fake-success).

kind.description: Geometry discriminator. Aligned with the v1 dwg_geometry_extract contract plus IR extensions.

default_start_width.description: AcDb2dPolyline default/constant start width applied where a vertex's own start_width is 0.

default_end_width.description: AcDb2dPolyline default/constant end width applied where a vertex's own end_width is 0.

vertices.description: Ordered vertices for polyline-family geometry. Each vertex is at minimum a point; LWPOLYLINE vertices may carry a bulge.

block_name.description: For block_reference geometry: referenced block name.

entity.description: A single drawing entity, normalized across engines. handle is the join key; class/dxf_name give the runtime and DXF type; owner_handle ties block-definition entities to their BTR; space says model/paper/block; geometry carries coordinates. This is a superset of the v1 dwg_geometry_extract entity shape.

object_id.description: Optional engine-local object id/pointer (non-portable; handle is the portable key).

dxf_name.description: DXF entity name (e.g. LINE, LWPOLYLINE, INSERT, DIMENSION).

owner_handle.description: Handle of the owning BTR (model space, paper space, or a block definition). Empty string allowed when owner is not resolved.

xdata.description: XDATA blocks attached to this entity (per registered app).

reactors.description: Handles of persistent reactors attached to this entity.

source.description: Per-entity provenance: which engine/pass emitted this record and any decode notes. Required so a partial/merged IR can attribute each entity.

diagnostics.description: Extraction self-report and the truth gate. entity_count is the asserted modelspace/total entity count; it MUST equal the realized entities[] length for the declared scope (this is the cross-engine truth gate, e.g. 21747 == 21747 == 21747). warnings/errors capture degradation; coverage enumerates which IR sections were actually populated by which engine.

entity_count.description: Asserted entity count for the declared scope (see count_scope). Truth-gate numerator.

realized_entity_count.description: Length of entities[] as actually emitted. A consumer/validator asserts entity_count == realized_entity_count for the matching scope; a mismatch is a hard failure.

entities_by_type.description: Count per DXF type (e.g. LINE, INSERT, LWPOLYLINE). Sums to entity_count for the matching scope.

coverage.description: Which IR sections were populated, and at what fidelity. Keys are IR section names (header_vars, layers, block_definitions, xdata, dictionaries, custom_objects, ...); values describe extraction status for that section.

sections_present.description: Names of IR sections this extractor populated.

sections_skipped.description: Names of IR sections deliberately not populated at this coverage level.

proxy_or_undecoded_count.description: Entities/objects recorded as present but not natively decoded (no-fake-success accounting).

engines.description: When this IR merges multiple extraction passes, the list of engines that contributed and the count each asserted (supports the 3-way agreement record).
